name: Recruitment Opportunities Table
source: Five Parsecs from Home Core Rules p.51
description: Table for determining recruitment task outcomes and potential crew members

1.narrative: Despite searching, no one suitable is interested in joining your crew

2.description: Found potential recruit for next turn
2.narrative: You find someone interested but they need time to consider the offer

3.description: Found basic recruit willing to join
3.narrative: A willing individual with basic skills agrees to join your crew

4.narrative: You recruit someone with valuable experience and abilities

5.narrative: An experienced specialist agrees to join your team

6.narrative: You recruit an exceptional individual who brings their own gear

basic.description: Standard recruit with basic equipment and random background

skilled.description: Recruit with a beneficial background and better equipment

exceptional.description: Elite recruit who brings additional equipment

SAVVY.description: Savvy characters are better at evaluating potential recruits

5_members.description: Larger crews are harder to expand

6_members.description: Very large crews have difficulty finding compatible members

BUSY.description: Busy worlds have more potential recruits

TRADE_HUB.description: Trade hubs attract travelers looking for work

FRONTIER.description: Frontier worlds have desperate individuals seeking opportunity

CORPORATE.description: Corporate worlds have fewer independent contractors

recruitment_costs.description: Standard cost to recruit new crew member

crew_limit.rule: Maximum crew size is typically 6 members
crew_limit.description: Most ships cannot support more than 6 crew members

background_compatibility.rule: Some backgrounds may create crew tension

equipment_requirements.rule: New recruits may need additional equipment
equipment_requirements.description: Budget for outfitting new crew members
